claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my daughter Alex, she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have one daughter, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation a bit better.

**Do you live with Alex, or does she live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's helpful.

Now, I need to check a few more important details about your circumstances.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status in the UK?** (This relates to your residency and immigration status in the UK.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Birmingham.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that. Just to clarify a bit further:

**What is your nationality, or do you have settled status in the UK?** (For example, are you a British citizen, or do you have another form of legal residence status in the UK?)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's very helpful.

Now I need to check one more important detail:

**Is Alex in full-time education, or is she in any other situation such as hospital care or residential care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She's just in regular primary school - nothing unusual like hospital or residential care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are there any other children living with you that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit, or is Alex your only child?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility. Let me review the Child Benefit eligibility rules to make my determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news!** You **are eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:
- ✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- ✓ You live with her in the UK
- ✓ You are responsible for her
- ✓ You are a British citizen
- ✓ She is in regular primary school education with no complications

All the key eligibility requirements are met. You should now be able to make a claim for Child Benefit. If you have any questions about how to apply or need further assistance, you can contact HMRC or visit the relevant government website.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) She is under 16 years old (age 8); (2) You live with her in the UK (Birmingham); (3) You are responsible for her and live with her; (4) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency requirement; (5) She is in regular primary school education with no complications such as hospital or residential care; (6) All the basic eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for your daughter Alex. You meet all the key requirements: you are a British citizen living in the UK, you live with Alex, she is under 16 years old, and she is in regular education. There are no complications such as hospital care or residential care that would affect her eligibility. You should be able to make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex."}
